item.title: Markets Have Two Rhythms: What 14 Stocks Taught Me About Windows and Memory
item.description:
Every spectral method faces the same question. Link to heading

How much history should you use?

Too short, and noise eats your signal. Too long, and you’re trading last year’s market.

Most people pick a number and move on. I spent two years asking the data instead.

Here’s what I found — and why it changed how I think about market structure.

The Setup Link to heading

I applied a Laplace‑based system identification to 14 stocks (high‑growth, large‑cap, crypto‑exposed — the usual suspects) using hourly data from 2024 to 2026.

item.title: About
item.description:
Duaity Link to heading

Duality is an independent research project developing SPECTRUM — a physics-informed framework for financial market dynamics, combining non-equilibrium thermodynamics, differential geometry (Einstein-Cartan), and entropy dynamics. Founded by Jeffee Hsiung, a KU Leuven graduate with research background at IMEC in radar 3D imaging and few-shot deep learning. Currently focused on bridging physical principles with financial modeling.

🔬 Research Philosophy Link to heading

Duality believes that financial markets, despite their apparent randomness, exhibit underlying geometric and spectral structures that can be uncovered through rigorous mathematical analysis. The current work focuses on:

item.title: Contact
item.description:
📬 Let’s Connect Link to heading

I’m always interested in connecting with fellow researchers, quantitative analysts, and anyone passionate about the intersection of mathematics and markets.

📧 Email

jeffee.hsiung@icloud.com

For research discussions, collaboration inquiries, or thoughtful questions about my work.

💼 LinkedIn

Duality

item.title: What Fourier Reveals About Markets: Unsupervised Clustering of 15 Stocks by Spectral Signature
item.description:
Empirical Validation — Spectral Clustering (March 2026) Link to heading

Purpose: Validate a physics‑inspired spectral approach against real market data across a diverse set of 14 symbols.

Spectral Cluster Analysis Link to heading

Figure 1: Hierarchical clustering (correlation distance) applied to the full power spectral densities of 14 stocks. Seven stable clusters emerge, recovering known sector/thematic groupings without using any sector labels. For example, crypto‑exposed assets (COIN, MSTR) show distinct high‑frequency tails, while mega‑cap tech (META, MSFT, NVDA, TSLA) share a common low‑frequency dominant period near 10 trading days.
